title: Leaf Space partners with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test connectivity service
summary: Leaf Space has partnered with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test a new space connectivity service called TreeNet, which aims to improve seamless space communications. The service was unveiled on May 27 by the Italian ground segment operator.

title: Astronauts briefly shelter in Dragon during ISS leak repair
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station were instructed to briefly shelter in a Dragon spacecraft while cosmonauts repaired an air leak in a Russian module. The incident occurred on June 5.

title: Next Ariane 6 launch to carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites using upgraded boosters
summary: The next Ariane 6 launch will carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites, marking the largest number of broadband satellites flown to date by Amazon. This mission will also be the first to use upgraded boosters.

title: Let’s build the moon base, but not lose sight of Mars
summary: NASA and the space industry are experiencing significant advancements, including the Artemis 2 crew's successful lunar flyby. The focus remains on building a moon base while maintaining Mars as a long-term goal.

title: Qianfan constellation deployment hits 200 satellites with Long March 8 and 6A launches
summary: The Qianfan constellation, led by Shanghai, reached 200 satellites with the deployment of two new launches using Long March 8 and 6A rockets. This follows two recent experimental flights and accelerates the constellation's construction.

title: Satellite maker Apex’s valuation rises to $2.3 billion after latest $200 million raise
summary: Apex, a satellite manufacturer, raised $200 million in a funding round, increasing its valuation to $2.3 billion. The company plans to expand its in-house satellite production capabilities with the new funds.

title: The Exploration Company completes drop test of Nyx capsule
summary: The Exploration Company successfully conducted a drop test of its Nyx spacecraft, designed for cargo transport to low Earth orbit. This test is a precursor to a planned 2028 test flight.

title: Axiom Space adds more than $175 million to funding round
summary: Axiom Space raised over $175 million in its latest funding round, with Japan’s largest bank joining as an investor.

title: HASC NDAA markup challenges Space Force on satellite programs
summary: The House Armed Services Committee (HASC) is challenging the U.S. Space Force's decisions on satellite programs in the NDAA markup, seeking to preserve a missile-warning satellite program the Pentagon aims to cancel. It also criticizes recent tactical communications satellite procurement and GPS oversight.

title: AstroForge completes DeepSpace-2 spacecraft
summary: AstroForge, an asteroid mining startup, has finished assembling its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft for a launch later in 2024, incorporating improvements after a previous mission failure.

title: Going supersonic! NASA's X-59 jet breaks sound barrier for the 1st time
summary: NASA's X-59 jet successfully broke the sound barrier for the first time on June 5, achieving a top speed of Mach 1.1. This milestone marks a significant step in supersonic flight research.

title: DC Studios lights the fuse on a sensational final 'Supergirl' trailer and it's totally peak (video)
summary: DC Studios released the final trailer for the upcoming 'Supergirl' project, teasing a dramatic conclusion. The trailer highlights differing perspectives on heroism between the main characters.

title: Inside the cruel confines of Apple TV's 'Star City' with star Rhys Ifans and series creators (interview)
summary: Apple TV's 'Star City' explores danger on Earth rather than in space, starring Rhys Ifans and created by the team behind 'For All Mankind'. The series contrasts the risks of space exploration with terrestrial threats.

title: Scientists find wind blowing from our Milky Way's black hole after half-century search: 'There it is'
summary: Astronomers have discovered powerful winds emanating from Sagittarius A*, the supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way, after a 50-year search. This breakthrough provides new insights into the behavior of black holes and their influence on galactic environments.

title: 'Masters of the Universe' is spacier than we expected (and it's awesome)
summary: The article highlights the surprising space-related elements in the movie 'Masters of the Universe' and lists its 10 most notable space-themed moments. It praises the film's space-centric aspects as impressive and enjoyable.

title: Astrophotographer captures colossal 'Godzilla' plasma cloud stalking the edge of the sun (video)
summary: An astrophotographer captured a massive solar prominence resembling a 'Godzilla' plasma cloud on the sun's edge, showcasing coronal rain flowing along magnetic field lines.

title: Astronauts on International Space Station take shelter in SpaceX Dragon as cosmonauts try to fix air leak
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station took shelter in a SpaceX Dragon capsule due to an air leak on the Russian side of the station. Cosmonauts worked to fix the persistent leak while the crew remained safe.

title: Meteor streaks across the sky above big observatory | Space photo of the day for June 5, 2026
summary: A meteor streaked across the night sky above Arizona's Kitt Peak National Observatory, creating a spectacular visual display. The event was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 5, 2026.

title: $1.77 trillion! SpaceX is about to become the 7th-most valuable American company
summary: SpaceX is set to become the 7th-most valuable American company with a $1.77 trillion valuation following its IPO, where shares will be sold at $135 each.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions despite the loss of its New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives following the anomaly.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: The U.S. Space Force is assessing damage caused by an anomaly and identifying future risk areas. Space Launch Delta 45, which manages the Eastern Range, has cleared a significant hurdle in its evaluation.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the mission, though timelines and landers remain uncertain.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week, with six missions planned from five different providers. The launches highlight the growing competition in the satellite internet sector.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The explosion marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, focusing on rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative builds on existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news source TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific space industry news content is provided for analysis.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing materials to withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust and precise space technology, though specific details are not provided.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a spacecraft parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test is likely part of preparations for a future space mission.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of past or present life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate the ability to rendezvous with an asteroid. This mission sets the foundation for future asteroid mining efforts planned before the end of the decade.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised $200 million in a funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. This marks the third time the company has secured a $200 million round within approximately 14 months.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The U.S. is expanding its commercial space station initiatives to Europe as the ISS nears the end of its operational life. This move ensures continued European access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 recap highlights a differentiated revenue model compared to hardware-focused companies. The discussion emphasizes Planet's approach to mitigating binary outcomes in its business operations.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company successfully completed a drop test for its Nyx spacecraft on May 19. The test evaluated parachute performance and vehicle dynamics during a controlled Earth landing.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a planned July launch. The satellite marks a significant milestone for Austria's space industry.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times larger than its predecessor, Condor-XL. The new satellite bus is designed for the emerging orbital data-center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to revert to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement strategy. The move aims to align with industry feedback and reshape the path forward.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations before the end of the year after assessing damage to its launch infrastructure. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight timeline on social media.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating two Pentagon offices, the Space Development Agency (SDA) and the Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO), which are focused on accelerating space acquisition. This move could significantly impact the Pentagon's efforts to modernize and streamline space capabilities.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a waiver to deploy 50% of its Project Kuiper constellation by the end of July, but with conditions on spectrum priority. This decision impacts Amazon's satellite broadband plans.

title: Apex Hits $2.3B Valuation With Latest Funding Raise
summary: Apex Space, a satellite bus manufacturer, raised an additional $200 million in capital, bringing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This funding round more than doubles the company's previous valuation.

title: Aalyria to Support Orchestration for NASA SCaN Program
summary: NASA has selected Aalyria to support orchestration for its Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) Program, beginning with the PExT demonstration mission. The PExT mission, Polylingual Experimental Terminal, aims to advance communication technologies in space.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite services for maritime applications. This collaboration aims to enhance connectivity solutions in the maritime sector.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million contract option for maritime domain awareness using AI-enabled data analytics. This extends a previous contract for unclassified analytics services to U.S. Combatant Commands.

title: Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom & Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission
summary: Infinite Orbits has selected Open Cosmos to develop two satellites, 'Tom & Jerry', for a maneuvering and inspection mission in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO). The mission aims to demonstrate space rendezvous capabilities.

title: Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure
summary: Mbryonics and NTT have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on developing a high-speed optical communications infrastructure for space. The partnership aims to enhance space communications capabilities.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform named 'Ultra' aimed at supporting orbital data centers. The first mission for this platform is scheduled for 2025.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of satellite propulsion systems.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully launched an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company is an aerospace and defense firm.
